name: Certifications as filters, not footnotes
logic: In procurement, a certification isn't a trust badge in the footer — it's a hard gate. 'Must be FSC.' 'Must be EN 13432 compostable.' 'Must be food-contact safe.' When compliance is a filterable attribute, you turn a buyer's non-negotiable constraint into a one-click path to exactly the compliant products — and turn an objection ('do you even have…?') into a funnel. Buyers expect to filter by the attributes that actually drive their decision; in B2B, certifications sit at the top of that list.

evidence.title: E-Commerce Product List Usability — Report & Benchmark
evidence.note: Baymard: buyers expect to filter by the attributes that drive their decision. Applied here to compliance certifications BY EXTENSION — no certification-specific study cited, hence partial.

exemplars.name: Grainger compliance filters
exemplars.why: Lets buyers filter MRO products by compliance and standards attributes.

metricsNote: Evidence is by extension from general filtering research — no certification-specific study. Flagged partial; verify before using in a pitch.
